items.title: VI Live: Ajax aast op revanche na slachtpartij tegen AZ
items.summary: AZ neemt het zondagmiddag om 14.30 uur andermaal op tegen Ajax. De Alkmaarders stonden een kleine maand geleden ook al tegenover Ajax, dat zich wil revancheren voor de pijnlijke 6-0 nederlaag in de Eurojackpot KNVB Beker. Volg de Eredivisie-ontmoeting tussen AZ en Ajax in deze VI Live.

items.title: Palmer weerspreekt transfergeruchten en gaat dieper in op blessures
items.summary: Cole Palmer velde eigenhandig Wolverhampton Wanderers met een hattrick in de eerste helft. In de Engelse media gonsde het van de geruchten dat hij bij Chelsea zou vertrekken. Dat sprak hij bij BBC Match of the Day tegen. Ook had de Engels international het over zijn blessureleed.

items.title: VI Live: kraker tussen Liverpool en Man City in Engeland
items.summary: Zondag staat ons een fraaie voetbaldag te wachten. De duels van Feyenoord, Ajax en PSV volgen we in aparte liveblogs, maar in deze VI Live blijf je op de hoogte van alle andere duels. Bijvoorbeeld van de Engelse topper tussen Liverpool en Manchester City.

items.title: De keeper die na een dubbele nekbreuk in de Champions League debuteerde
items.summary: In Balverliefd aandacht voor de gang van zaken achter de schermen van het voetbal en opvallende gebeurtenissen aan de rand van het veld. Deze week over de Nederlandse Club Brugge-keeper met een engeltje op zijn schouder.

items.title: ‘We lived a miraculous thing’: Castel di Sangro, 30 years on from their epic rise
items.summary: Small town club’s Serie B adventure captivated football and inspired a famous book. That spirit remains and is being passed to their successors The WhatsApp group flickers into life at about 6am every day. It is the manager who goes first because, when you are 79, old habits die hard. “Good morning,” Osvaldo Jaconi hails his former players and staff before, little by little, the salutations roll in from across Italy. Maybe it is someone’s birthday or another special occasion; the conversation may be accelerated by an in-joke that recalls why, three decades ago, they were brought together in the first place. Just in case anyone could forget, the group’s title says: “Serie B.” This is how miracles stay alive. Perhaps it is the point of what Castel di Sangro achieved in 1995-96. A rag-tag bunch from this backwater in mountainous Abruzzo had risen from local amateur leagues and then, in a crowning triumph with little precedent, made it to the second tier. “It’s like 30 years haven’t passed,” says Angelo Petrarca, who was nominally the masseur but often resembled a one-man backroom. “It shows how much love everybody has for each other, and did back then. As if everybody is still right here.” Continue reading...

items.title: ‘It has changed my life’: Wrexham’s Hollywood takeover, five years on
items.summary: When Ryan Reynolds and Rob Mac became club guardians in 2021 the Premier League was a dream. Now it’s a target Two Chewbaccas handed out flyers to passersby. No one making their way towards the Turf batted an eyelid, but then again, for five years now, a touch of Hollywood has become pretty much the norm in Wrexham. Ninety minutes before kick-off the city’s most famous public house was heaving. Lying in the shadow of the Racecourse Ground, it is the watering hole of choice for locals, and, thanks to landlord Wayne Jones’s prominent role in Welcome to Wrexham, the Netflix documentary following the club’s many fortunes, a tourist attraction. Continue reading...

items.title: Bosz kan Happel evenaren bij succesvol bezoek aan de Euroborg
items.summary: PSV-trainer Peter Bosz kan zondag bij een overwinning op FC Groningen Ernst Happel evenaren op de lijst van trainers met de meeste zeges in de Eredivisie achter hun naam. Bosz bezet momenteel de negende plaats op die rangschikking.

items.title: How Ekitike has already become a Liverpool cult hero
items.summary: Inside the rise of Hugo Ekitike, adapting to life in England, connecting with Florian Wirtz and why he's already a fan favourite.

items.title: Liverpool signing Jacquet suffers 'serious' injury
items.summary: Liverpool 's new summer signing Jérémy Jacquet has sustained a "serious" shoulder injury, according to Rennes head coach Habib Beye.

items.title: Foxborough demands clarity on $8M WC funding
items.summary: Town officials in Foxborough, Massachusetts, are seeking clarity on the source of around $8 million they say is needed to fund public safety for this summer's World Cup games at Gillette Stadium, the home of the New England Patriots.

items.title: Wrexham hero Mullin hits out at club after exit
items.summary: Paul Mullin has hit out at parent club Wrexham, saying he's "never going to accept" the way he was frozen out of the team over the past 18 months.

items.title: Messi scores solo golazo in Miami preseason draw
items.summary: Lionel Messi scored a trademark solo goal and added an assist for new signing Germán Berterame as Inter Miami drew 2-2 with Barcelona SC in a preseason friendly in Quito, Ecuador, on Saturday.

items.title: Ball loses WBA featherweight title after brutal knockout defeat to Figueroa
items.summary: Nick Ball lost his WBA featherweight title after a knockout defeat to Brandon Figueroa in front of his home crowd in Liverpool on Saturday.

items.title: 'I'm not doing my job well enough' - but is Howe now under pressure?
items.summary: Newcastle boss Eddie Howe admitted he is "not doing my job well enough" after Saturday's defeat by Brentford - but is he under pressure?

items.title: Eddie Howe facing ‘harsh reality’ after Newcastle’s home loss to Brentford
items.summary: ‘I think I’ve got to do better, I’ve got to do more’ Ouattara’s late goal gave Brentford 3-2 victory Eddie Howe said he was facing a “harsh reality” and felt “angry” with himself after watching his Newcastle team lose 3-2 at home to Brentford on Saturday evening. It was their fourth defeat in five games in all competitions and left the Saudi Arabian-owned club 12th in the Premier League. A soundtrack of boos greeted the final whistle but Newcastle’s manager did not complain. Continue reading...

items.title: Real Madrid step up Rodri interest - Sunday's gossip
items.summary: Real Madrid are stepping up interest in Manchester City midfielder Rodri, Arsenal are among clubs keen on Leon Goretzka and Manchester United and Newcastle target Michael Kayode, plus more.

items.title: England cement status as France’s chief Six Nations rivals
items.summary: If France set the benchmark with their emphatic opening‑night victory over Ireland, then England proved they are the main rivals to their Six Nations crown.

items.summary: Welcome to the most unlikely Super Bowl in history. At least, that's what the odds tell you.

items.title: Are West Ham single-handedly making relegation battle interesting?
items.summary: BBC Sport looks at how things are shaping up as the fight for Premier League survival hots up.

items.title: Scottish Cup round-up: Celtic require extra time to book QF spot
items.summary: Celtic needed extra time to advance to the quarter-finals of the Scottish Cup after a dramatic 2-1 come-from-behind victory over Dundee in front of a half-empty Celtic Park as St Mirren, Dunfermline and Partick Thistle also progressed to the last eight.
